questions.q: Why did Devavrata swear his terrible oath?
options: To gain a boon of invincibility in battle
To win Satyavati as a bride for his lovesick father
To be named crown prince of Hastinapura
To free his mother Ganga from a curse
questions.explanation: Devavrata renounced everything so that his father Shantanu could marry Satyavati, whose father demanded the throne for her sons.

questions.q: What two things did Devavrata give up by his oath?
options: His claim to the throne and all married life
His weapons and his warrior duties
His name and his place in the Kuru family
His palace and his royal treasury
questions.explanation: He renounced the throne of Hastinapura and swore lifelong celibacy so that no son of his could ever claim the crown.

questions.q: What name did Devavrata earn the day he swore the oath?
options: Devapi
Bhishma
Vyasa
Chitrangada
questions.explanation: The terrible nature of the vow earned Devavrata the name Bhishma, by which he is known for the rest of the epic.

questions.q: What does the name Bhishma broadly signify?
options: He who conquers all directions
He who never speaks falsehood
He of the terrible oath
He who is beloved of the gods
questions.explanation: Bhishma means the terrible or awe-inspiring one, given because of the fearsome oath of celibacy and renunciation he swore.

questions.q: According to the epic, how did the heavens respond at the moment the oath was sworn?
options: The sky darkened and thunder rolled
Indra appeared and offered a chariot
A divine voice forbade the marriage
The gods rained flowers and cried 'Bhishma!'
questions.explanation: Celestial flowers fell from the sky and divine voices proclaimed 'Bhishma!' in wonder at the severity of the vow.

questions.q: Whose demand drove Devavrata to swear the oath?
options: Satyavati's father, the chief of the fishermen
King Shantanu, who feared a succession war
Satyavati herself, who distrusted princes
The sage Parashara, guarding his son's rights
questions.explanation: The fisher chief refused to give Satyavati in marriage unless her future sons, not Devavrata, would inherit the throne.

questions.q: What boon did Shantanu grant Devavrata in gratitude for the sacrifice?
options: Mastery of every celestial weapon
Sovereignty over half the kingdom
The power to choose the hour of his own death
Freedom from all curses and sins
questions.explanation: A moved Shantanu granted his son ichchha-mrityu, the power to die only at the moment of his own choosing.

questions.q: Who was the lovesick father for whose sake the oath was taken?
options: King Pratipa
King Shantanu
King Kuru
King Pandu
questions.explanation: Shantanu, king of Hastinapura, pined for the fisher-girl Satyavati, and his son's oath made the marriage possible.

questions.q: What position did Devavrata hold at the time he renounced it?
options: Commander of the border garrisons
Chief priest of the royal court
Regent ruling for an infant king
Anointed heir apparent of Hastinapura
questions.explanation: Devavrata had already been installed as yuvaraja, the heir apparent, before he gave up his claim for his father's happiness.

questions.q: Which woman did Shantanu long to marry, setting the oath in motion?
options: Satyavati, the fisher chief's daughter
Ganga, the river goddess
Amba, the princess of Kashi
Kunti, the princess of Kuntibhoja
questions.explanation: Shantanu fell in love with the fragrant Satyavati, and her foster father's harsh condition led directly to the vow.

questions.q: What condition had the fisher chief set that Shantanu himself could not accept?
options: That Shantanu abdicate at once in his favor
That Satyavati be crowned a ruling queen
That the sons born of Satyavati inherit the throne
That Devavrata be exiled from the kingdom
questions.explanation: Shantanu could not disinherit his worthy son Devavrata, so he returned home pining rather than agree to the condition.

questions.q: After Devavrata renounced the throne, why did the fisher chief still hesitate?
options: He doubted a prince would keep his word
He feared Devavrata's future sons would claim the crown
He wanted a bride price of gold and cattle
He insisted the king himself come to ask
questions.explanation: The chief pointed out that even if Devavrata stood aside, his children might contest the throne, prompting the further vow of celibacy.

questions.q: Where did Devavrata go to swear his oath before witnesses?
options: The royal court of Hastinapura
The hermitage of the sage Vasishtha
The temple of Shiva outside the city
The fisher chief's home by the Yamuna
questions.explanation: Devavrata travelled to the dwelling of the fisher chief on the banks of the Yamuna and swore the oath before the assembled fisherfolk.

questions.q: How did Devavrata first learn the cause of his father's strange melancholy?
options: He questioned the king's aged charioteer and ministers
Ganga appeared and revealed it to him in a dream
Satyavati sent him a secret message
Vyasa arrived at court and announced it
questions.explanation: Noticing Shantanu's wasting sorrow, Devavrata pressed the king's old charioteer and counselors, who revealed the story of Satyavati.

questions.q: What did Devavrata do immediately after swearing the vow?
options: He retired to the forest to begin austerities
He crowned the fisher chief as a vassal king
He drove Satyavati on his chariot to his father as a new mother
He sent messengers to summon the kingdom's nobles
questions.explanation: Having sworn the oath, Devavrata himself escorted Satyavati on his chariot to Hastinapura and presented her to Shantanu.

questions.q: Years later, after Vichitravirya died childless, what did Satyavati beg Bhishma to do?
options: Adopt a son from a neighboring royal house
Marry the widows and sire heirs, setting the vow aside
Conquer new lands to strengthen the weakened line
Perform a great sacrifice to win sons from the gods
questions.explanation: Satyavati pleaded with Bhishma to wed the widowed queens and continue the dynasty, but he refused to break his vow.

questions.q: How did Bhishma answer when urged to break his vow to save the dynasty?
options: He agreed on condition the gods absolved him
He asked Vyasa to release him from the oath
He demanded the throne in return for compliance
He said he could renounce the worlds but never his truth
questions.explanation: Bhishma declared he might give up the three worlds or anything else, but he would never abandon the truth of his oath.

questions.q: Because of the oath, who became king of Hastinapura after Shantanu's death?
options: Chitrangada, Satyavati's elder son
Vichitravirya, Satyavati's younger son
Bhishma, ruling only as caretaker king
Dhritarashtra, the blind prince
questions.explanation: With Bhishma barred by his vow, the crown passed to Satyavati's elder son Chitrangada, exactly as the fisher chief had demanded.

questions.q: Since the vow barred Bhishma from fathering heirs, who finally sired sons on Vichitravirya's widows?
options: Parashara, by Satyavati's request
A Vasu descended for the purpose
Vyasa, summoned by his mother Satyavati
Shantanu, restored to life by a boon
questions.explanation: Satyavati summoned her firstborn son Vyasa, who fathered Dhritarashtra, Pandu, and Vidura through the practice of niyoga.

questions.q: What lifelong duty did the oath effectively bind Bhishma to?
options: Performing the royal sacrifices each year
Serving and protecting whoever sat on Hastinapura's throne
Leading every military campaign in person
Training all princes of the realm in archery
questions.explanation: Having renounced kingship, Bhishma pledged himself to serve and defend the throne of Hastinapura no matter who occupied it.

questions.q: Why did Bhishma abduct brides for Vichitravirya instead of contesting a swayamvara for himself?
options: His vow of celibacy barred him from ever taking a wife
Custom forbade an elder brother to marry late
He considered the assembled kings unworthy rivals
Satyavati had forbidden him to enter the contest
questions.explanation: Bound to lifelong celibacy by his oath, Bhishma could only win brides on behalf of his young half-brother, never for himself.

questions.q: What did the boon attached to the oath mean for Bhishma's mortality?
options: No weapon forged by man could wound him
He would live exactly one hundred years
His life was tied to the Kuru throne's safety
Death could take him only when he himself willed it
questions.explanation: Shantanu's boon of ichchha-mrityu meant Bhishma could be wounded but could not die until he chose the moment himself.

questions.q: In which book of the Mahabharata is the oath of Devavrata narrated?
options: The Sabha Parva
The Adi Parva
The Udyoga Parva
The Shanti Parva
questions.explanation: The story of Shantanu, Satyavati, and Devavrata's terrible oath belongs to the Adi Parva, the epic's first book.

questions.q: By what name is Satyavati's foster father, the fisher chief who exacted the oath, commonly known?
options: Uparichara
Hiranyavarman
Dasharaja
Sthunakarna
questions.explanation: The chief of the fishermen is commonly called Dasharaja in tradition, while Uparichara Vasu was Satyavati's birth father.

questions.q: Per the Adi Parva, how long had Devavrata been heir apparent when Shantanu met Satyavati?
options: Four years
Twelve years
One year
Sixteen years
questions.explanation: The Adi Parva says four years had passed since Devavrata's installation as heir apparent when Shantanu encountered Satyavati.

questions.q: The oath's lifetime of celibate exile on earth also fulfilled an older curse upon whom?
options: Shantanu, cursed by the sage Durvasa
Ganga, cursed to live among mortals
The Kuru line, cursed by King Yayati
The Vasu Dyaus, cursed by the sage Vasishtha
questions.explanation: Bhishma was the Vasu Dyaus, condemned by Vasishtha to a long mortal life without offspring, a fate the vow brought to pass.

questions.q: By what popular Sanskrit phrase is the terrible oath itself remembered?
options: Satya Vachana
Bhishma Pratigya
Dridha Sankalpa
Maha Shapatha
questions.explanation: The oath is famously called the Bhishma Pratigya, a phrase still used for any unbreakable vow.

questions.q: What did Devavrata declare he would still attain despite dying without sons?
options: A rebirth in the solar dynasty
Liberation from the cycle of rebirth
The everlasting regions of heaven
Fame outlasting the Kuru name
questions.explanation: Swearing his celibacy, Devavrata declared that even sonless he would win the eternal regions of heaven through his vow.

questions.q: When the rejected princess Amba demanded Bhishma marry her, why did the oath make tragedy inevitable?
options: He had already promised her to Shalva
Satyavati forbade a Kashi alliance
Custom barred an abductor from marrying his captive
He could not wed her without breaking his vow, so he refused
questions.explanation: Bhishma's celibacy left Amba with no husband and no recourse, turning her into the instrument of his eventual fall.

questions.q: How did Shantanu's boon, given for the oath, shape Bhishma's final days?
options: He lay on the bed of arrows, holding off death until the sun turned north
He died instantly the moment Shikhandi's arrow struck him
He gave up the boon to revive the fallen Abhimanyu
He chose to die the day Duryodhana was crowned
questions.explanation: Using the power to choose his death, Bhishma lay wounded on the bed of arrows and waited for the auspicious Uttarayana before dying.
